message:
feat: add evidence pack viewer and experiment panel E2E tests

- Add /sessions/[threadId]/evidence route with lab-mode gating
- Evidence pack viewer displays records, excerpts, verification status
- Add Evidence Pack link to session detail page header
- Create E2E tests for experiment panel happy path workflow
- Tests properly skip when environment not configured

Closes: brenner_bot-5so.13.4.1, brenner_bot-5so.13.4.2, brenner_bot-5so.13.4
Closes: brenner_bot-5so.12.3.3, brenner_bot-5so.12.3

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.3","title":"Task: Implement experiment post (encode + send DELTA message)","description":"# Goal
Optionally collapse encode+send into one operator command.

---

# Proposed UX
```bash
./brenner.ts experiment post \
  --thread-id RS-... \
  --sender OperatorName \
  --to BlueLake,PurpleMountain,RedForest \
  --test-id T1 \
  --result-file results.json
```

This should:
- run the encoder
- send a DELTA message with subject `DELTA[results]: ...`

---

# Safety
- Posting is a side effect; require explicit command.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `experiment post` sends a correct DELTA message to the thread
- [ ] Errors do not lose result files (fail loud)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.4","title":"Task: E2E test: run experiment → encode → post → compile","description":"# Goal
Prove the full “results loop” works end-to-end on a tiny fixture command:
run → capture → encode → send → compile.

---

# Test flow (v0)
1) Start a session thread (can be a local test thread id)
2) Run an experiment via the wrapper (no sandbox assumption)
3) Encode the resulting `ExperimentResult` JSON into DELTA blocks
4) Post the DELTA to the thread (or simulate by injecting the message body into the compiler input)
5) Compile and assert the artifact reflects the run metadata

---

# Notes
- Prefer a *minimal* command that is cross-platform and deterministic.
- Avoid leaving behind large files; results should live under a test-scoped temp dir or a small fixture folder.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Test covers the full pipeline with minimal mocking
- [ ] Fails loudly if any step silently drops data

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2.5","title":"Task: Update runbook for experiment-backed sessions","description":"# Goal
Add a copy/paste operator workflow for experiment-backed sessions.

---

# Include
- Session workspace best practice (`--project-key` / run in the repo/folder you’re studying)
- How to define a discriminative test command (T#)
- How to run it with `experiment run` (+ `--timeout`)
- Where result files are written and how to inspect them
- How to `experiment encode` → DELTA blocks
- How to `mail send` the DELTA message
- How to `session compile` / `session publish`

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A future operator can run the loop without inventing any commands
- [ ] Runbook explicitly distinguishes “capture” vs “interpretation”

-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3","title":"Feature: Web lab experiment panel (run + view + post results)","description":"# Goal
Make experiment-backed sessions usable from the web **lab UI** (optional v0+), not just the CLI.

---

# UX sketch
Within a session page (`/sessions/[threadId]`) in **lab mode**:
- operator can run a declared test command (or paste one)
- operator can see stdout/stderr + exit code + provenance
- operator can convert the run into DELTAs and post them into the thread

---

# Security / safety constraints
- **Lab mode only** (fail closed)
- Same auth requirements as other orchestration actions
- Execution is always explicit operator action
- Default stance: wrapper-run + capture (not a hardened sandbox)

---

# Why this is P3
CLI is the primary path for real research workflows. The web panel is a convenience layer once the CLI loop is proven.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Web UI can trigger an experiment run (via server-side API)
- [ ] Results are viewable and auditable (provenance obvious)
- [ ] Posting results creates DELTA messages in the thread

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.1","title":"Task: Add API route to run experiments (wrapper-run + capture)","description":"# Goal
Add a server-side API endpoint that triggers an experiment run using the same underlying runner as the CLI.

---

# Requirements
- Accept `project_key` + `thread_id` + `test_id` + command
- Execute via the same wrapper-run implementation as `brenner experiment run`
- Return structured result payload (and/or persist a result file and return its path)

---

# Security
- Require lab mode + orchestration auth
- Treat this as “operator clicked a button”, not autonomous execution

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] API returns stdout/stderr/exit code/duration + provenance
- [ ] API fails loudly on missing inputs

Added 12 unit tests covering auth, validation, and execution.","labels":["api","experiments","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:05:44.767444598-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.3.2","title":"Task: Session page experiment panel UI (results viewer + post)","description":"# Goal
Add an experiment panel to the session page that:
- runs an experiment command
- shows the captured result (stdout/stderr/exit code) with provenance
- can post encoded DELTAs back into the thread

---

# UX notes
- Make provenance obvious (command, timestamp, cwd/project).
- Encourage discriminative framing: link results back to T# and H#.
- Keep output safe to render (truncate, avoid HTML injection).

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Experiment panel visible in lab mode
- [ ] Results viewer shows stdout/stderr safely
- [ ] "Post DELTAs" creates a DELTA message in the thread

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.4","title":"Feature: One-command cockpit start (ntm + roster + kickoff)","description":"# Goal
Reduce the operator overhead of starting a multi-agent session.

---

# Problem
Today, operators do many manual steps:
- pick thread id
- spawn ntm session
- compose excerpt
- send kickoff
- tell agents to check mail

This is doable, but high-friction.

---

# Desired UX
A single command should get us to "agents are working" with minimal copy/paste:

```bash
brenner cockpit start \
  --thread-id RS-... \
  --roster-preset default-3-agent \
  --excerpt-file excerpt.md \
  --question "..."
```

It may:
- spawn ntm session (optional flag)
- send kickoff messages (role-separated)
- broadcast "check mail" to all panes
- print next commands (status/compile)

---

# Constraints
- Remain humans-in-the-loop (no attempt to drive external CLIs programmatically).
- Avoid hidden side effects; provide `--dry-run` option.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `brenner cockpit start` exists (or equivalent)
- [ ] Works with roster mapping (deterministic roles)
- [ ] Has a dry-run mode that prints commands without executing

Lock the command surface for `brenner cockpit start`.

- default behavior: dry-run vs execute
- how to specify ntm layout / pane counts
- how to pick recipients (from roster preset vs explicit)

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CLI flags documented with examples

- [ ] Sends kickoff via Agent Mail
- [ ] Broadcasts "check mail" template to panes

@@ -395,6 +395,9 @@ export default async function SessionDetailPage({
refreshed {now} + + Evidence Pack + New Session
